key_results.turquoise_core_ora: Top-100/200 kME: 0 significant GO terms. Top-500 kME: 1 GO-CC term (p.adj<0.05). Whole-module ORA was underpowered; core-only marginally better.
key_results.kme_ranked_gsea: GSEA found significant pathways missed by ORA. Turquoise: mitochondrial respiratory chain assembly (NES=-2.80, p.adj=0.047), mitochondrial translation (NES=-2.71, p.adj=0.043), motile cilium assembly (NES=+2.39). Blue: mitochondrion organization (NES=-2.83), energy derivation by oxidation (NES=-2.55), lymphocyte mediated immunity (NES=+2.55). Brown: results present. Green: results present.
key_results.ecm_score_correlation: ECM score (153 genes) vs module eigengenes: MEgreen r=+0.69 p<0.001, MEturquoise r=+0.41 p<0.001, MEbrown r=-0.43 p<0.001, MEgrey r=+0.62 p<0.001, MEblue r=-0.17 ns, MEyellow r=-0.02 ns.
key_results.signed_network: Signed network produced 7 modules (vs 5 unsigned). Turquoise persists as a distinct module. Signed network confirms turquoise is not an unsigned-network artifact. Two additional modules (black, red) emerged from splitting.

warnings: KEGG failed for turquoise-core ORA (SSL connect error to rest.kegg.jp) - network issue, not data issue
Signed network trait correlations not generated (script wrote module-module correlation matrix instead) - minor, unsigned trait correlations from round 1 still valid

Turquoise GSEA results: mitochondrial pathways are negatively enriched (genes at bottom of kME ranking), meaning low-kME turquoise genes are mitochondrial. This is expected - turquoise is a large module with mixed functions.
